summary: Cleaned up the lb refract module by collapsing several split lbrefract assembly/data fragments into one asm/melee/lb/lbrefract.s, replacing src/melee/lb/lbrefract3.c with a cleaner src/melee/lb/lbrefract.c, and updating build/config metadata to treat lbrefract as one unit. The new C file contains cleaned implementations for atan2f, acosf, asinf, atanf, and a local inverse-square-root helper, using explicit bitwise float handling, lookup-table constants, and MetroTRK intrinsics where needed for matching.
what_changed: Added a single consolidated asm/melee/lb/lbrefract.s containing the lbrefract text and data that had previously been split across lbrefract1.s, lbrefract2.s, lbrefract3.s, lbrefract_data.s, and lbrefract_shared_001.s.
Deleted the old split assembly files asm/melee/lb/lbrefract1.s, asm/melee/lb/lbrefract2.s, asm/melee/lb/lbrefract3.s, asm/melee/lb/lbrefract_data.s, and asm/melee/lb/lbrefract_shared_001.s.
Deleted src/melee/lb/lbrefract3.c and added src/melee/lb/lbrefract.c with cleaned C implementations for atan2f, acosf, asinf, atanf, and static helper lbRefract_80022DF8.
Updated configure.py from separate NonMatching objects for lbrefract_data.c, lbrefract1.c, and lbrefract2.c to a single NonMatching object for melee/lb/lbrefract.c.
Updated obj_files.mk to replace several individual lbrefract object entries with one $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/lb/lbrefract.s.o entry.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt so atanf_lookup remains at .rodata:0x803B7300 but is annotated with data:float.
smart_moves: Reduced object-file fragmentation by making lbrefract a single module in both the assembly layout and build configuration.

Kept fragile matching details explicit in the cleaned C: float bit reinterpretation macros, sign-bit checks, exact bit-pattern threshold constants, and MetroTRK intrinsics such as __frsqrte and __fnmsubs.
Replaced many magic external float labels from lbrefract3.c with clearer standard math constants and local macros where the evidence supported it, such as M_PI, M_PI_2, BITWISE_PI_2, NAN, and INF.
Converted the arctangent lookup data into a static const float atanf_lookup table in source and recorded the symbol metadata as data:float, improving readability and future tooling/type recovery.
Preserved known symbol names such as lbRefract_80021CE8, lbRefract_8002219C, lbRefract_PObjLoad, and lbRefract_80022DF8 while improving file organization.
decomp_lessons: When a module has accumulated multiple temporary asm fragments, consolidating them into one module-level asm file can simplify obj_files.mk and configure.py without changing symbol boundaries.
For math-library decomp work, exact float bit patterns and sign handling may be clearer and safer as named macros than as scattered extern float labels.
Lookup tables should get explicit symbol metadata such as data:float when known; this helps future decompilation and searchability.
Do not prematurely hide matching-sensitive operations: intrinsics like __frsqrte and __fnmsubs, bitwise float casts, and explicit polynomial/lookup indexing are useful evidence for exact codegen.
Replacing a numbered file such as lbrefract3.c with the canonical module name lbrefract.c makes the source tree align better with the original .file "lbrefract.c" seen in assembly.
naming_conventions: Canonical module source name: src/melee/lb/lbrefract.c instead of numbered lbrefract3.c.
Module function names retain the lbRefract_800xxxxx address-based convention where semantic names are not known.
Local helper lbRefract_80022DF8 remains static and address-named because no stronger semantic name is evidenced.
Bit-pattern macros use uppercase names such as SIGN_BIT, BITWISE_PI_2, BITWISE_INF, BITWISE_THRESHOLD_0, and GET_SIGN_BIT.
Lookup table name atanf_lookup is preserved and typed as float in symbols.txt.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used __frsqrte followed by three Newton-Raphson refinement steps in lbRefract_80022DF8, matching the inverse-square-root pattern visible in the assembly.
Used BITWISE(f), SIGNED_BITWISE(f), and GET_SIGN_BIT(f) macros to preserve exact IEEE-754 sign-bit behavior in atan2f and atanf.
Used exact hexadecimal float bit constants for thresholds and classifications in atanf, including BITWISE_0_5, BITWISE_1_0, BITWISE_2_0, and BITWISE_INF.
Used __fnmsubs in atanf's lookup-based range reduction to preserve fused negative multiply-subtract code generation.
Kept the atanf polynomial evaluation explicitly ordered and protected with clang-format off comments, suggesting expression shape mattered for readability or matching.
Used MSL_TrigF_80400770 and MSL_TrigF_80400774 arrays as sources for NAN and INF rather than inventing new constants.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that fully match or rename lbRefract_80022DF8, lbRefract_80021CE8, or lbRefract_8002219C.
Search for subsequent changes to atanf_lookup or the atan2f/atanf matching strategy.
Check whether later PRs split lbrefract.s back into source functions or replace more assembly with C.
